data.title: Merck KGaA Non-GAAP EPS of €2.12, revenue of €5.28B; updates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Merck KGaA press release (OTCPK:MKGAF): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of €2.12.
  • Revenue of €5.28B (+3.1% Y/Y), up 2.5% organically compared with the year-earlier quarter.
  • EBITDA pre up organically by 5.8% to €1.5 billion.
  • The company now expects Group full-year net sales of between €20.9 billion and €22.4 billion and EBITDA pre of between €5.8 billion and €6.4 billion. This corresponds to organic sales growth of 2% to 6% and an organic EBITDA pre growth of 2% to 7%.

data.title: Mizuho Financial GAAP EPS of ¥350.20, revenue of ¥9030.37B; initiates FY outlook
data.description:
• Mizuho Financial press release (NYSE:MFG): FY GAAP EPS of ¥350.20.
  • Revenue of ¥9030.37B (+3.3% Y/Y).

data.title: ENGIE SA reports Q1 results; reaffirms FY25 outlook
data.description:
• ENGIE SA press release (OTCPK:ENGIY): Q1 Revenue of €23.25B (+5.6% Y/Y).
  • EBIT excluding nuclear at €3.7B, an organic increase of 2.1%, mainly driven by Infrastructures and favourable timing effect.
  • Cash Flow From Operations1 at €4.0B in Q1 2025
  • Maintaining a solid balance sheet with an economic net debt/EBITDA ratio down to 3.0x.
  • Economic net debt reduced by €1.8B.
  • FY 2025 guidance confirmed, with NRIgs expected in a range of €4.4-5.0B, with EBIT excluding nuclear in an indicative range of €8.0-9.0B.

data.title: Cisco in charts: Q1 product revenue up 15% Y/Y, driven by 8% growth in Networking and 54% surge in Security
Deutsche Telekom AG Non-GAAP EPS of €0.50, revenue of €29.75B; raises FY outlook
data.description:
• Deutsche Telekom AG press release (OTCQX:DTEGY): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of €0.50.
  • Revenue of €29.75B (+6.5% Y/Y).
  • In organic terms, net revenue increased by 3.8 %. Service revenue was up 6.3 % to €25.0 billion. In organic terms, net revenue increased by 3.5 %.
  • Adjusted EBITDA AL grew by 7.9 % to €11.3 billion. In organic terms, it increased by 5.3 %.
  • Cash capex (before spectrum investment) decreased from €4.7 billion to €4.3 billion.
  • Free cash flow AL was up by €1.9 billion to €5.6 billion.
  • Guidance raised for the 2025 financial year: We are raising our guidance for adjusted EBITDA AL and free cash flow AL of the Group for the 2025 financial year. Instead of around €44.9 billion, we now expect to post adjusted EBITDA AL of around €45.0 billion. Instead of the previous level of around €19.9 billion, we now expect to post free cash flow AL of around €20.0 billion.

data.title: Tesla chairwoman Robyn Denholm made $198M in last six months
U.S. authorizes $304M missile sale to Turkey amid efforts to boost ties
data.description:
The U.S. has approved the sale of $304M in missiles to Turkey, as the NATO allies seek to bolster their trade and defense relationship.

The State Department on Wednesday made a determination approving a possible foreign military sale to the Republic of Turkiye of AIM-120C-8 Advanced Medium-Range Air-to-Air Missiles and related elements of logistics and program support for an estimated cost of $225M.

According to the release, the Turkish government had requested to purchase 53 AIM-120C-8 Advanced Medium Range Air-to-Air Missiles and six AIM- 120C-8 AMRAAM guidance sections.

Additionally, the department approved the sale of 60 AIM-9X Sidewinder Block II All Up Round missiles, 11 AIM-9X Block II Tactical guidance units, and various logistical and program support services. This portion of the deal is valued at $79.1M.

The principal contractor will be RTX Corporation (NYSE:RTX), located in Tucson, Arizona.

“This proposed sale will support the foreign policy goals and national security of the United States by improving the security of a NATO Ally that continues to be a force for political and economic stability in Europe,” the department said.

The deal, which still requires approval from Congress, came as Secretary of State Marco Rubio's arrived in Turkey for a NATO foreign ministers' meeting on Thursday.

data.title: Siemens Non-GAAP EPS of €3.00, revenue of €19.76M; reaffirms FY outlook
data.description:
• Siemens press release (OTCPK:SIEGY): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of €3.00.
  • Revenue of €19.76M (+7.0% Y/Y).
  • Second-quarter orders rose 10% year-over-year to €21.6 billion.
  • Book-to-bill ratio of 1.10.
  • On a comparable basis, excluding currency translation and portfolio effects, orders were up 9%, and revenue rose 6%.
  • Profit Industrial Business surged 29% to €3.2 billion on strong operational execution and benefited from a €0.3 billion gain related to exiting a business at Smart Infrastructure; profit margin Industrial Business was 16.9%.
  • Free cash flow from continuing and discontinued operations was €1.0 billion.
  • Outlook: "Despite increased uncertainty in the economic environment, we confirm our outlook for fiscal 2025. Digital Industries expects for fiscal 2025 a change in comparable revenue, net of currency translation and portfolio effects, in a range of (6)% to 1% and a profit margin of 15% to 19%. Smart Infrastructure expects for fiscal 2025 comparable revenue growth of 6% to 9% and a profit margin of 17% to 18%, excluding a gain of €315 million, recorded in Q2 FY 2025 from exiting its wiring accessories business. Mobility expects for fiscal 2025 comparable revenue growth of 8% to 10% and a profit margin of 8% to 10%. For the Siemens Group, we expect comparable revenue growth in the range of 3% to 7% and a book-to-bill ratio above 1."
  • "We expect basic EPS from net income before purchase price allocation accounting (EPS pre PPA) for fiscal 2025 in a range of €10.40 to €11.00, excluding a positive €2.64 per share related to the sale of Innomotics. For comparison, EPS pre PPA in fiscal 2024 was €10.54 excluding a positive €0.61 per share from Siemens Energy Investment. This outlook excludes burdens from legal and regulatory matters. Effects related to the acquisition of Altair, which closed at the end of Q2 FY 2025, are not yet included. "

data.title: France inflation stagnant at 0.80% in April
data.description:
The inflation rate in France remained unchanged at 0.80% in April.

The Consumer Price Index in France increased 0.60% in April over the previous month. 

This price increase is mainly explained by the increase in the prices of services (+1.1% after stability), driven by the strong rebound in those of transport services (+10.2% after -2.8%), and a further increase in the prices of "other services" (+0.7% after +0.3%). Food prices increased again over one month (+0.7% after +0.3%), those of manufactured goods marked time (+0.1% after +1.1%), and those of energy fell for the third consecutive month (-1.6% after -1.5%).

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWQ)

Currency: (EUR:USD)

data.title: The Westaim GAAP EPS of -$0.34, revenue of $4.7M
data.description:
• The Westaim press release (OTCPK:WEDXF): Q1 GAAP EPS of -$0.34.
  • Revenue of -$4.7M (vs. $28.8M last year, same quarter).
  • Book value per fully diluted share1 was $22.56 (C$32.43) at March 31, 2025, compared to $22.88 (C$32.90) at December 31, 2024.
